bio.education: North Mason High School (Belfair, Wash.) ‘13 Washington State University ‘18, Kinesiology and Psychology; LIFE University ’23, Chiropractic (D.C.)
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Son of Jeffrey and Amy Allen
  • Has one brother, Corey
  • Co-leads bible studies for athletes and coaches
  • Is in the process of developing a YouTube channel
  • Cooking is a passion of his and he could see myself owning his own restaurant someday
  • Hobbies include cooking, camping and producing YouTube videos
World Championships Experience

  • Top finish: 10th – 2023 (400m Hurdles - Men)
Olympic Experience

    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, 10th (400m Hurdles - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Was a setter at Cal State Northridge
  • Married to fellow volleyball player Janelle Ruen. The couple has one son
  • In 2018, published a book called Good Blood, The Descendants of Terene
Career Highlights
  • Has four international podium finishes, all on the NORCECA Continental Tour
  • Made his professional beach debut in 2004 on the domestic AVP Tour
  • ---
  • DID YOU KNOW?
  • In 2018, played in five FIVB events with Ryan Doherty. Best finishes were ninth in The Hague and Doha. Placed fourth with Doherty at NORCECA in Boca Chica. Placed second with Doherty at AVP Hermosa Beach and third in New York and San Francisco
  • In 2017, played in four FIVB events, finishing 17th three times with Stafford Slick; earned bronze at NORCECA Punta Cana with Slick and on the domestic AVP Tour, had five podium finishing including one first-place
  • Played in four FIVB tournaments in 2016, top career finish was ninth in Long Beach with Theo Brunner
  • Made his FIVB debut with Trevor Crabb at the 2015 Long Beach Grand Slam, duo finished 33rd
  • In 2016, earned one bronze and two silver with Stafford Slick on the NORECA Tour
  • First international tournament was in 2014 on NORCECA
  • Between 2004-16 on the AVP Tour, has one gold and four bronze - gold came in 2016 with Theo Brunner
  • Made his professional debut at 2004 AVP Huntington Beach Open
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – 9th (Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Team - Men 2019
  • Top finish: 9th – 2019 (Team - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Son of Cathy and Tom Allen
  • Has three siblings: Chris, Lisa and Kaylee
  • Drafted by the Oakland Athletics in the third round of the 2017 MLB June Amateur Draft
  • Has created a brand for himself called Dangrszn
Career Highlights
  • 2019 CAL Mid-Season All-Star
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (Team - Men)
More
NGB Bio

Son of Louis and Joey Allen. Has a fraternal twin sister, Carissa, along with one other sister, Rikki, and a brother, William. Played football at the University of Oregon in addition to running track for the Ducks. At wide receiver, played two seasons at Oregon and reached the 2015 College Football Playoff final. Hauled in a career-high seven touchdowns during the 2014 season. Sustained a torn ACL on New Year’s Day 2015 in the Rose Bowl while returning a kickoff against Florida State. Passed up a potential professional football career. Was baptized two days before punching his ticket to Rio 2016 on the Willamette River in Eugene, Oregon.

Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, 4th (110m Hurdles - Men)
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 5th (110m Hurdles - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2017 – 9th (110m Hurdles - Men)
  • Years of participation: 110m Hurdles - Men 2017
  • Top finish: 9th – 2017 (110m Hurdles - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Was competing in wheelchair basketball at an adaptive sports tournament in Houston, when her final match was cancelled
  • The event also had an air rifle competition where the winner would get the opportunity to attend a training camp at the Olympic and Paralympic Training Center in Colorado Springs
  • She entered and won, and decided to pursue shooting
  • At age 18 her spinal cord became affected by progressive myelopathy, which resulted in paralysis in her lower back and her needing the use of a wheelchair at age 22
  • Much of her inspiration comes from her daughter, Rebekah, who was born in July 2015
  • Parents are Richard Almlie and Brenda Case
  • Married to Matthew Ryan
  • Hobbies include playing all wheelchair sports.
Paralympic Experience
  • 3-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Paris 2024, 26th (R4 - 10m Air Rifle Standing SH2 - Open), 27th (R5 - 10m Air Rifle Prone SH2 - Open)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, 16th (R5 - 10m Air Rifle Prone SH2 - Open), 17th (R4 - 10m Air Rifle Standing SH2 - Open)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 17th (R4 - 10m Air Rifle Standing SH2 - Open), 23th (R5 - 10m Air Rifle Prone SH2 - Open)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2022 – 14th (R5 - 10m Air Rifle Prone SH2 - Open), 37th (R4 - 10m Air Rifle Standing SH2 - Open)
  • Years of participation: R4 - 10m Air Rifle Standing SH2 - Open 2014, 2018, 2019, 2022; R5 - 10m Air Rifle Prone SH2 - Open 2014, 2018, 2019, 2022
  • Top finish: 14th – 2022 (R5 - 10m Air Rifle Prone SH2 - Open)

entries.para_classification: R4 (Mixed 10m Air Rifle Standing SH2), R5 (Mixed 10m Air Rifle Prone SH2, Falling Target Rifle SH2)

bio.biography:
Career Highlights

  • 2011 Pan American Games, gold - MVP
  • 2010 IBAF World Junior AAA U-18 Championship, 1st
  • 2011 USA Baseball Athlete of the year

Quick Facts
  • Son of Albert and Ana Almora
  • Selected by the Chicago Cubs in the first round (6th overall) of the 2012 MLB Draft.

Career Highlights
  • Pan American Games, Toronto, 2015 (Team - Women)
  • World Cup, Edmonton, 2012 (Team - Women)
  • World Cup, 2010 (Team - Women)
  • World Cup, Matsuyama, 2008 (Team - Women)
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Julio and Ofelia Alvarez
  • Has one sibling
  • Played softball at Villanova University
  • Played for the Miami Stingrays and in Spain for El Club Antorcha de Valencia in 2006
  • Is a firefighter in Hollywood, Florida
  • Hobbies include paddle boarding, fishing, boating, running and photography.

Quick Facts
  • She rode the elevator with Michael Phelps two times
  • She stepped on Michelle Obama's foot when she hugged her on a visit to the White House
  • She has put a nose clip on John Legend
  • Competed in 2012 U.S. Olympic Team Trials as a 14 year old, and she was there for 11:11 on 11/11/11
  • Her mom competed in the 1984 U.S. Olympic Trials with her duet and placed 7th
  • Her grandpa was in charge of all the off ice officials in the 1980 Olympic Games, and he was the time keeper of the 'Miracle on Ice' hockey game in Lake Placid, NY
  • Her dad has coached two Olympians but not in the sports they became Olympians in
Olympic Experience
  • 3-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, silver (Team - Open)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, 13th (Duet - Women)
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 9th (Duet - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – 11th (Team Free Routine - Women), 12th (Duet Free Routine - Women, Duet Technical Routine - Women, Solo Free Routine - Women)
  • Years of participation: Duet Free Routine - Women 2015, 2017, 2019; Duet Technical Routine - Women 2015, 2017, 2019; Solo Free Routine - Women 2019; Team Free Routine - Women 2015, 2017, 2019; Team Technical Routine - Women 2015
  • Top finish: 10th – 2015 (Team Technical Routine - Women)

Quick Facts
  • I like that synchro combines elements from lots of different sports such as swimming and gymnastics, which is actually why I joined in the first place.
  • I’ve also met a lot of my friends through synchro, and I love that it gives the opportunity to compete at a national level.
  • Favorite Synchronized Swimmer: Virginie Dedieu
  • Along with synchro, I am also part of my high school’s swim team that competes in the fall. This year we won our 15th state championship and I placed 2nd in the 100 back and 5th in the 200 IM.
Career Highlights
  • 2018 China Open: Team Tech - 2nd, Team Free - 2nd
  • 2017 Swiss Open: Team - 2nd
  • 2017 Junior All-Star Team
  • 2017 US Nationals: Team - 4th, Duet - 9th, Solo - 11th
  • 2016 Junior All-Star Team
  • 2016 US Nationals: Junior Duet - 8th, Senior Duet - 6th
  • 2015 Junior and 13-15 All Star Teams
World Championship Experience
  • 2016 Junior World Championships: Team - 9th

Quick Facts
  • Parents are David and Laurie Aman
  • Has two sisters, Jordan (1993) and Addie (1995)
  • Ben most admires his grandpa because, “He started from nothing and showed that hard work and dedication will always succeed. He is in his 70’s now and he is still working hard and proving that you never give up on your dreams, no matter how old you are.”
Career Highlights
  • 2016: ParaVolley Intercontinental Cup (6th Place)
  • 2013: ParaVolley Pan American Sitting Volleyball Zonal Championships in (Silver)
  • DID YOU KNOW:
  • Joined the U.S. Men’s Sitting Volleyball Team in 2012
  • Lost the front half of his right foot after getting it caught under a lawn mower when he was five years old
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 8th (Team - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2022 – 6th (Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Team - Men 2014, 2022
  • Top finish: 6th – 2022 (Team - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Scott and Joanne Andersen

  • Has one sister, Morgan

  • On top of track and field, she also played soccer in high school

  • Placed third at the 2019 USA Championships in hammer throw

  • Finished second at the 2018 NCAA Division I Championships in hammer throw

  • Most recent: 2023 – 25th (Hammer Throw - Women)
  • Years of participation: Hammer Throw - Women 2023
  • Top finish: 25th – 2023 (Hammer Throw - Women)

bio.education: Marple Newtown High School (Newtown Square, Penn.) ’14 DeVry University
bio.biography:
Quick Facts

  • Sport: Curling

  • Position: Third

  • Specialty: Women’s Curling, Mixed Doubles

Biographical Information

Sarah Anderson is a curler from Pennsylvania who was introduced to curling at a young age. Her parents Wayne and Arlene Anderson are originally from Winchester, Ontario, and grew up curling. Sarah, her twin sister Taylor Anderson-Heide, and three other sisters (Larissa Day, Courtney Slata, and Emily Pinelli) all followed in their parents’ footsteps, taking up curling. Anderson has competed alongside her twin sister Taylor in several events. 

Olympic Highlights

Sarah competed in the Inssubruck 2012 Youth Olympic Games in Austria, where she placed fifth with her mixed-gender curling team.

World Championship Highlights

Anderson won a silver medal at the 2016 World Junior Curling Championships.

U.S. Championship Highlights

In 2019, Sarah won the U.S. Women’s Curling Championship with her twin sister, Taylor Anderson. She also won gold at the U.S. Mixed Doubles Championships in 2015 and 2018.

Career Highlights
  • World Cup, Suzhou, 2019 (Doubles - Mixed)
  • World Cup, Beijing, 2019 (Doubles - Mixed)
  • World Cup, Omaha, 2019 (Team - Women)
  • World Junior Championship, Tårnby, 2016 (Team - Women)
  • World Junior Championship, Tallinn, 2015 (Team - Women)
Early Career

Sarah began her curling career by competing at the 2012 Winter Youth Olympic Games in Innsbruck, Austria, where she placed fifth in the mixed-gender curling event.

World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2022 – 5th (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2013, 2019, 2022; Doubles - Mixed 2015, 2018
  • Top finish: 4th – 2013 (Team - Women)

bio.education: Granite Bay High School (Granite Bay, Calif.) '09 University of Southern California ’13, Communications
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Randall and Colette Anderson
  • Colette swam at University of Hawaii
  • Has one older sister, Alyssa, and one younger sister, Jordan, both are swimmers
  • Alyssa won gold at the Olympic Games London 2012 in the 4x200 freestyle
  • Competed in soccer, track and field, and cross-country before committing to swimming
  • Hobbies include reading, watching cooking and home renovation TV shows and spending time with her dog, Baxter
  • Appeared on “Hell’s Kitchen” in 2016
  • Most decorated female open water swimmer in American history, with eight international medals, entering Tokyo 2020.
Career Highlights
  • First American to earn an Olympic medal in open water swimming
Olympic Experience
  • 3-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, 6th (10km Freestyle - Women)
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 5th (10km Freestyle - Women)
    • Olympic Games London 2012, silver (10km Freestyle - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – silver (10km Freestyle - Women), bronze (5km - Team Relay - Mixed)
  • Years of participation: 10km Freestyle - Women 2015, 2017, 2019; 5km - Team Relay - Mixed 2017, 2019; 400m Freestyle (SC) - Women 2018; 800m Freestyle (SC) - Women 2018; 5km Freestyle - Women 2013, 2015, 2017; 5km - Team Time Trial - Mixed 2013; 25km Freestyle - Women 2010, 2011; 1500m Freestyle - Women 2009; 800m Freestyle - Women 2009
  • Medals: 5 (2 gold, 2 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Gold – 2015 (5km Freestyle - Women); 2013 (5km Freestyle - Women)
    • Silver – 2019 (10km Freestyle - Women); 2017 (5km - Team Relay - Mixed)
    • Bronze – 2019 (5km - Team Relay - Mixed)
More
USA Swimming

Quick Facts
  • Began swimming at age 4 and quickly began competing. Born with osteogenesis imperfecta, which limits the range of motion in her lower limbs. Hallie says she enjoys the way it feels when she swims and that nothing is holding her back
  • Daughter of Tim and Heather Anderson
  • Hobbies include reading and learning
  • Favorite subjects are math and science.
Career Highlights
  • ParaPan American Games, Lima, 2019 (100m Breaststroke SB9 - Women)
